title: T-023 — GS1-128 barcode parser service (WH §7.4, §15.2)
prompt:
# T-023 — GS1-128 barcode parser service (WH §7.4, §15.2)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §7.4, §15.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement shared `barcode-parser-service.ts` extracting AIs from GS1-128 / FNC1 / parentheses-format barcodes: (01) GTIN-14, (10) lot, (17) expiry YYMMDD, (11) production date YYMMDD, (37) count, (310x) net weight kg. <100 ms P95. Falls back to manual on parse error.

## Implementation contract
1. `packages/barcode/src/gs1-128-parser.ts`: exported `parseGs1128(input: string): ParsedGs1Result` returning `{ais: {gtin?, lot?, expiry?, manufactureDate?, count?, netWeightKg?}, raw: string, format: 'fnc1'|'parens'|'unknown', errors: string[]}`.
2. Supports both FNC1 (`\u001d`-separated) and parentheses (`(01)0123...(10)BATCH`) formats.
3. (17) and (11) parsed as YYMMDD → ISO date (1990-2089 window).
4. (310x) decimal indicator digit drives decimal placement.
5. Errors collected (not thrown) so the wrapper service can audit-log parse failures.

## Files
- packages/barcode-parser/src/gs1-parser.ts [create]
- packages/barcode-parser/src/__tests__/gs1-parser.test.ts [create]
- packages/barcode-parser/package.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given input `'(01)09506000123450(10)ABC123(17)260815'`, when parsed, then `{gtin:'09506000123450', lot:'ABC123', expiry:'2026-08-15'}`.
2. Given an FNC1 input with `\u001d` separators, when parsed, then the same AIs are extracted.
3. Given input `'(310x)123456'` with x=3, when parsed, then `netWeightKg=123.456`.
4. Given malformed input, when parsed, then `errors[]` is non-empty and ais are partial-or-empty, never throwing.

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/barcode/src/__tests__/gs1-128-parser.test.ts` covering FNC1, parens, malformed, decimal-indicator, leap-year boundary.
- Run `pnpm --filter barcode test gs1-128-parser`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not throw on parse errors — collect into `errors[]`.
- Do not hardcode decimal places for (310x) — read indicator digit.
- Do not skip the 1990-2089 century-rollover window for YYMMDD.
